control.objective: To ensure terminated or transferred employees have their Active Directory (AD) access revoked in a timely manner to prevent unauthorized access to organizational systems and data.
control.controlNature: Manual review control with system-generated evidence

auditMetadata.preparedForUseCase: RAG retrieval, reasoning agent testing, gap analysis, and control comparison

controlDesign.designUnderstanding: Based on discussions held with the IT Operations Manager, all requests impacting production systems are initiated through the ITSM tool and routed for CRB approval prior to implementation. The CRB reviews the business impact, rollback procedures, testing evidence, and security considerations before approving deployment.
designAttributes: Control owner performs review or approval activity
Evidence is retained in system workflow, report, screenshot, ticket, or approval record
Auditor validates timing, completeness, and appropriateness of evidence

testSteps: Obtained the population of production change requests during the audit period.
Selected a sample of changes for detailed testing.
Verified whether CRB approval was documented prior to deployment.
Reviewed implementation timestamps against approval dates.
Inspected supporting evidence including change tickets and approval records.
testing.testingPerformedNarrative: For sampled change request CHG-2026-1045, CRB approval was completed on April 12, 2026 at 2:15 PM while production deployment occurred on April 13, 2026 at 10:00 PM. Evidence reviewed included ServiceNow approval screenshots and deployment logs.
testingFocus: Consistency between source records and evidence

evidenceMapping.purpose: Supports auditor validation of control execution
Supports auditor validation of control execution
Supports auditor validation of control execution
Supports auditor validation of control execution

risk.riskStatement: Terminated or transferred employees may retain access to systems and data if access is not revoked timely.

auditConclusion.conclusionNarrative: Based on the procedures performed, the control appears to be suitably designed and operating effectively during the audit period.

control.objective: To ensure terminated or transferred employees have their Active Directory (AD) access revoked in a timely manner to prevent unauthorized access to organizational systems and data.
control.controlNature: Manual review control with system-generated evidence

auditMetadata.preparedForUseCase: RAG retrieval, reasoning agent testing, gap analysis, and control comparison

controlDesign.designUnderstanding: During walkthrough meetings with the Infrastructure Lead, management explained that production changes cannot proceed without documented CRB approval within the ServiceNow workflow. The review includes validation of testing results, implementation plans, and segregation of duties.
designAttributes: Control owner performs review or approval activity
Evidence is retained in system workflow, report, screenshot, ticket, or approval record
Auditor validates timing, completeness, and appropriateness of evidence

testSteps: Requested the quarterly change management register.
Selected sample production deployments.
Validated existence of CRB approval within the workflow.
Verified deployment dates occurred after approval completion.
Reviewed attached testing and rollback documentation.
testing.testingPerformedNarrative: Sample ticket CHG-2026-2088 related to a firewall configuration update was inspected. The audit team verified that CRB approval and security sign-off were completed prior to implementation into production.
testingFocus: Consistency between source records and evidence

evidenceMapping.purpose: Supports auditor validation of control execution

risk.riskStatement: Terminated or transferred employees may retain access to systems and data if access is not revoked timely.

auditConclusion.conclusionNarrative: No exceptions were identified during testing and the control was operating effectively.

controlDesign.designUnderstanding: As explained by the Change Manager during the audit walkthrough, change requests are presented to the CRB during weekly meetings where technical, operational, and security impacts are evaluated before approval is granted.
designAttributes: Control owner performs review or approval activity

testSteps: Obtained change tickets migrated into production.

testingFocus: Consistency between source records and evidence

control.objective: To ensure terminated or transferred employees have their Active Directory (AD) access revoked in a timely manner to prevent unauthorized access to organizational systems and data.
control.controlNature: Manual review control with system-generated evidence

auditMetadata.preparedForUseCase: RAG retrieval, reasoning agent testing, gap analysis, and control comparison

controlDesign.designUnderstanding: Based on discussions with the IT Infrastructure Manager, the organization uses Active Directory to centrally manage user accounts and authentication. HR communicates employee termination or transfer details to the IT support team through the HRMS system. Upon receiving notification, the IT administrator disables the user account within Active Directory and removes associated privileged access where applicable.
designAttributes: Control owner performs review or approval activity
Evidence is retained in system workflow, report, screenshot, ticket, or approval record
Auditor validates timing, completeness, and appropriateness of evidence

testSteps: Obtained the list of terminated employees during the audit period.
Selected a sample of users for testing.
Verified HR termination dates against AD account disablement dates.
Inspected screenshots and logs evidencing account disablement.
Verified whether privileged access was also revoked.
testing.testingPerformedNarrative: For sampled user ID EMP1045, the HR termination date was March 10, 2026, and the AD account was disabled on the same day. Screenshot evidence from Active Directory confirmed account disablement.
testingFocus: Consistency between source records and evidence

actualEvidenceInspected: ITSM tickets related to access revocation

Supports auditor validation of control execution

actualEvidence: ITSM tickets related to access revocation

auditConclusion.conclusionNarrative: Based on testing performed, AD access revocation controls appear to be operating effectively.

testingFocus: Consistency between source records and evidence
